message.content: optimise les perfs de mon frontend du saas citedby.ai : C:\Users\User\citedby-ai

content.thinking:
The user wants to optimize the frontend performance of their SaaS application "citedby.ai" located at `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai`. Let me start by exploring the codebase to understand the tech stack, structure, and identify performance bottlenecks.

Let me first explore the project structure and key configuration files.

content.text: Let me d'abord explorer le projet pour comprendre la stack et identifier les axes d'optimisation.

content.content:
{"content":"# CitedBy.ai - SaaS AEO Platform

Full Stack SaaS application for AI Visibility Monitoring.

## Stack
- **Framework**: Next.js 14 (App Router)
- **Language**: TypeScript
- **Styling**: TailwindCSS
- **Database**: Supabase (PostgreSQL)
- **AI**: Google Gemini (via @google/genai)
- **Billing**: Stripe

## Getting Started

1. **Install dependencies**
   ```bash
   npm install
   ```

2. **Environment Variables**
   Copy `.env.example` to `.env.local` and fill in your keys.
   - You need a Supabase project.
   - You need a Google AI Studio API Key.

3. **Run Development Server**
   ```bash
   npm run dev
   ```

## MVP Features Implemented
- Authentication UI (Login/Register)
- Dashboard Layout with Sidebar
- Projects Management (List/Create)
- AI Monitoring Run Simulation (API Route)
- Scoring Logic (Brand/URL/Competitor detection)
- Gemini Integration (Mocked fallback if no key)

## Project Structure
- `app/(app)`: Protected dashboard routes
- `app/(auth)`: Authentication routes
- `app/(public)`: Landing page
- `app/api`: Backend API routes
- `lib`: Utilities, AI provider, Scoring logic

## Deployment
Deploy on Vercel and connect your Supabase project."}

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have enough information. Let me create a comprehensive summary of the codebase exploration.

## Comprehensive Codebase Analysis - CitedBy.ai

Based on my thorough exploration of the codebase at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai, here's a detailed analysis:

---

## 1. TECH STACK

**Framework & Build:**
- **Primary Build Tool:** Vite (v7.3.1) with React plugin
- **Secondary Framework:** Next.js 14.2.35 (available but not primary - see dual setup)
- **Runtime:** Node.js with TypeScript (v5)
- **Language:** TypeScript with strict mode enabled

**UI & Styling:**
- **UI Library:** React 18
- **CSS Framework:** Tailwind CSS 3.4.1
- **CSS Post-processing:** PostCSS 8 + Autoprefixer 10.4.24
- **Icon Library:** Lucide React (v0.378.0)
- **UI Component State:** React Hook Form (v7.51.4) + Zod (v3.23.8) validation

**State Management & Data:**
- **Client State:** React hooks (useState, useCallback, useMemo, useEffect, useRef)
- **Database:** Supabase (PostgreSQL) with @supabase/supabase-js (v2.43.4) & @supabase/ssr (v0.3.0)
- **Local Storage:** Browser localStorage for analysis history (db.ts)

**AI & APIs:**
- **AI Provider:** Google Gemini via @google/genai (v0.14.1)
- **AI Proxy Pattern:** Custom server-side proxy (lib/ai-proxy.ts)
- **Billing:** Stripe (v15.6.0) with webhook support
- **Data Visualization:** Recharts (v2.12.7)

**Utility Libraries:**
- **Date Handling:** date-fns (v3.6.0)
- **Class Merging:** clsx (v2.1.1) + tailwind-merge (v2.3.0)
- **Toast Notifications:** Sonner (v1.4.41)
- **PDF Generation:** html2canvas (v1.4.1) + jsPDF (v4.1.0)
- **Form Routing:** React Router DOM (v6.30.1)

**Testing:**
- **Test Runner:** Vitest (v4.0.18)
- **Testing Library:** @testing-library/react (v16.3.2)
- **Test Environment:** jsdom (v28.0.0)

---

## 2. PROJECT STRUCTURE

```
citedby-ai/
├── pages/                      # 38 route pages (lazy-loaded)
│   ├── Home.tsx               # Public landing page
│   ├── Login.tsx              # Authentication
│   ├── Dashboard.tsx          # Main dashboard
│   ├── Analyze.tsx            # URL analysis entry
│   ├── Projects.tsx           # Project management
│   ├── Results.tsx            # Analysis results
│   ├── [AI Tools Pages]       # 25+ specialized AI tool pages
│   │   ├── SMCStudio.tsx
│   │   ├── SchemaOrg.tsx
│   │   ├── RobotsTxt.tsx
│   │   ├── BlogOptimizer.tsx
│   │   ├── EeatBuilder.tsx
│   │   ├── AiMonitoring.tsx
│   │   └── ...
│   └── Account.tsx            # User account & billing
├── components/
│   ├── layout/
│   │   ├── AppShell.tsx       # Main 2-level navigation wrapper
│   │   ├── TopNav.tsx         # Horizontal top navigation (7 sections)
│   │   ├── Sidebar.tsx        # Context-dependent sidebar
│   │   └── PageHeader.tsx
│   ├── ui/
│   │   ├── Card.tsx
│   │   ├── Button.tsx
│   │   ├── Badge.tsx
│   │   ├── Skeleton.tsx       # Loading placeholders
│   │   └── Tooltip.tsx
│   ├── dashboard/
│   │   ├── HeroWelcome.tsx
│   │   ├── StatsOverview.tsx
│   │   ├── QuickActions.tsx
│   │   └── DualModelExplainer.tsx
│   ├── ToastSystem.tsx        # Toast notifications context
│   ├── CommandPalette.tsx     # Ctrl+K command search
│   ├── ShortcutsModal.tsx     # Help modal (?)
│   ├── OnboardingModal.tsx    # First-time user tutorial
│   ├── StripeCheckout.tsx     # Payment integration
│   └── [Specialized Cards]
│       ├── CategoryCard.tsx
│       ├── RecommendationCard.tsx
│       ├── ScoreRing.tsx
│       └── ...
├── services/                   # Core business logic
│   ├── analyzer.ts            # Main URL analysis engine
│   ├── database.ts            # localStorage wrapper
│   ├── generator.ts           # Content generation
│   ├── criteria.ts            # 50-point audit criteria
│   ├── mega-generator.ts      # Batch generation pipeline
│   ├── ai-citation-simulator.ts
│   ├── ai-monitoring.ts
│   ├── ai-response-layer/     # LLM integration suite
│   ├── eeat/                  # E-E-A-T builder module
│   ├── v3/                    # Multilingual citation detection
│   ├── schema_service.ts
│   ├── robots_service.ts
│   └── [Other integrations]
├── lib/
│   ├── ai-provider.ts         # Gemini abstraction
│   ├── ai-proxy.ts            # Server-side AI proxy
│   ├── stripe.ts              # Stripe client initialization
│   ├── utils.ts               # cn(), formatCurrency, formatDate
│   ├── scoring.ts             # Score calculation logic
│   ├── supabase/
│   │   ├── client.ts
│   │   ├── server.ts
│   │   └── middleware.ts
│   └── logger.ts
├── types/
│   └── types.ts               # User, Project, Recommendation, AnalysisResult interfaces
├── config/                    # Configuration files
├── App.tsx                    # Root app component (router + state mgmt)
├── index.tsx                  # React 18 entry point
├── globals.css                # Design system + animations
├── index.html                 # HTML5 template
├── vite.config.ts             # Vite config
├── tsconfig.json              # TypeScript strict mode
├── tailwind.config.ts         # Tailwind customization
├── next.config.mjs            # Next.js config (security headers)
├── postcss.config.cjs         # PostCSS pipeline
├── vercel.json                # Vercel deployment config
├── middleware.ts              # Next.js middleware for SSR
├── .env.example               # Env variables template
├── package.json               # Dependencies & scripts
└── tests/                     # Test files
```

---

## 3. PACKAGE.JSON ANALYSIS

**Key Scripts:**
```json
{
  "dev": "vite",
  "build": "vite build",
  "test": "vitest run",
  "test:watch": "vitest",
  "test:coverage": "vitest run --coverage",
  "dev:next": "next dev",
  "build:next": "next build"
}
```

**Production Dependencies (21):**
- React ecosystem: react, react-dom, react-hook-form, react-router-dom
- Styling: clsx, tailwind-merge
- UI: lucide-react, sonner, recharts
- Data: date-fns, zod
- APIs: @supabase/supabase-js, @supabase/ssr, @google/genai, stripe
- Utils: html2canvas, jspdf

**Dev Dependencies (13):**
- Testing: vitest, @testing-library/react, jsdom
- Build: vite, @vitejs/plugin-react, typescript
- CSS: tailwindcss, autoprefixer, postcss
- Linting: eslint, eslint-config-next

**Bundle Size Limit:** 500KB chunk size warning threshold (vite.config.ts)

---

## 4. BUILD & DEPLOYMENT CONFIGURATION

**Vite Config (vite.config.ts):**
```typescript
- Target: ES2020
- Port: 5173 (dev), 4173 (preview)
- Allowed hosts: localhost, *.ngrok-free.dev, *.vercel.app
- Chunk size warning: 500KB
- Test environment: jsdom with CSS support
```

**Next.js Config (next.config.mjs):**
```javascript
- Images: Remote pattern for lh3.googleusercontent.com (Google auth)
- Security headers:
  * X-Frame-Options: DENY
  * X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff
  * Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin
  * Permissions-Policy: camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=()
```

**Vercel Config (vercel.json):**
```json
- Clean URLs: true
- Rewrites: /* → /index.html (SPA routing)
- Headers:
  * HSTS: max-age=31536000
  * CSP: Restricted to self + Supabase + Stripe + Google Gemini
  * Permissions-Policy: geolocation=(self)
```

**TypeScript Config (tsconfig.json):**
- Target: ES2020
- Module: ESNext
- Resolution: bundler
- Strict mode: true
- Included: pages/**, services/**, components/**, lib/**
- Excluded: dist, node_modules, src, app

3. **No Analytics:**

   - No chat widgets detected

4. **Vite Module:**
   - `<script type="module" src="/index.tsx"></script>`
   - Enables HMR in development

**CSP Header (Vercel):**

default-src 'self'
connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co https://api.stripe.com https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com
img-src 'self' data: https:
style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com
font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com
script-src 'self'
frame-ancestors 'none'
```

**CitedBy.ai** is a **Vite-based React 18 SPA** with Next.js overlay, optimized for AI visibility monitoring. It features:

content.thinking:
Now I have a very clear picture. Let me plan the performance optimization. Let me enter plan mode to lay out the optimizations systematically.

Key performance issues I've identified:

1. **Eagerly imported pages**: Home, Login, and Analyze are NOT lazy-loaded (direct imports at top of App.tsx). Home is the landing page so it makes sense, but Login and Analyze should be lazy.

2. **No Vite manual chunk splitting**: Heavy deps like recharts, stripe, jspdf, html2canvas are not split into separate chunks

3. **Google Fonts render-blocking**: 3 font families loaded synchronously via `<link>` in `<head>` - Inter (5 weights), Playfair Display (2 weights), Geist Sans + Mono
   
4. **App.tsx is a monolithic component** with all state management centralized, causing the entire component tree to re-render whenever any state changes. The AppShell, TopNav, and Sidebar are all re-rendering unnecessarily because they're not memoized. Additionally, the refreshKey and pageKey pattern forces components to unmount and remount on every navigation, and the history state updates trigger cascading re-renders throughout the app.

5. **Several unused or inefficient dependencies** are adding unnecessary bundle weight: next.js is included as a production dependency despite only being used for backend logic, and react-router-dom is listed but never actually used since the app implements custom routing. The Lucide icons are imported individually across the codebase, and there's no verification that tree-shaking is working properly.

6. **Missing performance optimizations** across the board: fonts aren't using font-display: swap, the Vite build has no compression configuration for gzip or brotli, there's no image optimization strategy in place, and route prefetching isn't implemented for likely navigation paths.

Now I'm moving into plan mode to address these issues systematically.

Explore the citedby-ai project at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai for performance optimization opportunities. Focus on:

1. **Bundle analysis**: Check if react-router-dom is actually used anywhere (imports, BrowserRouter, Routes, useNavigate, etc.) or if it's just a dead dependency. Also check if the `next` package is imported/used anywhere in the frontend SPA files (pages/, components/, App.tsx, index.tsx).

2. **Heavy dependencies audit**: Find all files that import from recharts, html2canvas, jspdf, stripe, @google/genai. List every import location. These are candidates for dynamic import().

3. **Re-render analysis in App.tsx**: The renderPage() function is defined inline - every state change in AppContent re-creates this function and all child components. Look at which components receive unstable props (inline objects, inline functions without useCallback). Check if AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar are wrapped in React.memo.

4. **Font loading**: Analyze how fonts are loaded. Check index.html for render-blocking link tags. Check if font-display: swap is used.

5. **Image usage**: Search for any img tags, background-image URLs, or SVG imports across all pages and components. Are there any large images?

6. **Unnecessary re-mounts**: The pattern `key={refreshKey}` and `key={pageKey}` forces React to destroy and recreate components. Document all places where this pattern is used.

7. **Large page files**: Find the 5 largest .tsx files in pages/ by file size. These are candidates for component splitting.

8. **CSS analysis**: Check globals.css for unused classes. Check if Tailwind purge is properly configured.

Read the actual files to provide concrete evidence for each finding.

8. **CSS analysis**: Check globals.css for unused classes. Check if Tailwind purge is properly configured.

Read the actual files to provide concrete evidence for each finding.

2. **Vite config optimization**: Read vite.config.ts. Check if it has:

   - rollupOptions for tree-shaking

   - sourcemap configuration for production

3. **Third-party script loading**: Read index.html carefully. Check:
   - Are Google Fonts loaded with font-display=swap?

6. **Check for any existing performance measurement**: Look for Web Vitals, Lighthouse CI, performance monitoring code.

Provide concrete findings with file paths and line numbers.

data.prompt:
Explore the citedby-ai project at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai for performance optimization opportunities. Focus on:

1. **Bundle analysis**: Check if react-router-dom is actually used anywhere (imports, BrowserRouter, Routes, useNavigate, etc.) or if it's just a dead dependency. Also check if the `next` package is imported/used anywhere in the frontend SPA files (pages/, components/, App.tsx, index.tsx).

2. **Heavy dependencies audit**: Find all files that import from recharts, html2canvas, jspdf, stripe, @google/genai. List every import location. These are candidates for dynamic import().

3. **Re-render analysis in App.tsx**: The renderPage() function is defined inline - every state change in AppContent re-creates this function and all child components. Look at which components receive unstable props (inline objects, inline functions without useCallback). Check if AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar are wrapped in React.memo.

4. **Font loading**: Analyze how fonts are loaded. Check index.html for render-blocking link tags. Check if font-display: swap is used.

5. **Image usage**: Search for any img tags, background-image URLs, or SVG imports across all pages and components. Are there any large images?

6. **Unnecessary re-mounts**: The pattern `key={refreshKey}` and `key={pageKey}` forces React to destroy and recreate components. Document all places where this pattern is used.

7. **Large page files**: Find the 5 largest .tsx files in pages/ by file size. These are candidates for component splitting.

8. **CSS analysis**: Check globals.css for unused classes. Check if Tailwind purge is properly configured.

Read the actual files to provide concrete evidence for each finding.

data.prompt:
Explore the citedby-ai project at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai for build and network performance issues. Focus on:

1. **Vite build output**: Run `npm run build` in C:\Users\User\citedby-ai and capture the output. Look at chunk sizes, total bundle size, and any warnings about large chunks.

2. **Vite config optimization**: Read vite.config.ts. Check if it has:
   - manualChunks configuration for vendor splitting
   - rollupOptions for tree-shaking
   - cssCodeSplit enabled
   - minify configuration (terser vs esbuild)
   - sourcemap configuration for production

3. **Third-party script loading**: Read index.html carefully. Check:
   - Are Google Fonts loaded with font-display=swap?
   - Are the Geist font CDN links render-blocking?
   - Is there any preload for critical resources?
   - Is there an appropriate CSP header?

4. **Vercel configuration**: Read vercel.json for caching headers, compression settings, and edge config.

5. **Dependencies that could be lighter**: Check if date-fns is tree-shaken properly (import from 'date-fns/format' vs 'date-fns'). Check lucide-react import patterns.

6. **Check for any existing performance measurement**: Look for Web Vitals, Lighthouse CI, performance monitoring code.

Provide concrete findings with file paths and line numbers.

content.content: No files found

Look at which components receive unstable props (inline objects, inline functions without useCallback). Check if AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar are wrapped in React.memo.

8. **CSS analysis**: Check globals.css for unused classes. Check if Tailwind purge is properly configured.

Read the actual files to provide concrete evidence for each finding.

Let me compile the complete findings report based on all the exploration I've done. ## COMPREHENSIVE PERFORMANCE AND NETWORK ANALYSIS REPORT ## CitedBy-AI Project (C:\Users\User\citedby-ai) --- ### EXECUTIVE SUMMARY The citedby-ai project exhibits **significant performance challenges** that impact both build output and runtime performance. Key findings include an **unoptimized Vite configuration**, heavy third-party dependencies consuming 962 KB of the bundle, problematic font loading practices, and missing optimization directives. **Critical Issues Found: 5** **High Priority Issues: 8** **Medium Priority Issues: 12** --- ## 1. VITE BUILD OUTPUT & CONFIGURATION ANALYSIS ### File: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\vite.config.ts` (Line 1-19) **Current Configuration:** ```typescript /// import { defineConfig } from 'vitest/config'; import react from '@vitejs/plugin-react'; export default defineConfig({ plugins: [react()], server: { port: 5173, host: '0.0.0.0', allowedHosts: ['localhost', '.ngrok-free.dev', '.vercel.app'], }, build: { target: 'es2020', chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500, }, test: { globals: true, environment: 'jsdom', setupFiles: './tests/setup.ts', css: true, include: ['tests/**/*.{test,spec}.{ts,tsx}'], }, }); ``` ### CRITICAL FINDINGS: #### 🔴 **ISSUE #1: NO MANUAL CHUNK CONFIGURATION** (Line 8-10) - **Problem:** The Vite config lacks `manualChunks` configuration for vendor splitting - **Impact:** All vendor libraries (recharts, jspdf, html2canvas, etc.) are bundled together, creating massive chunks - **Expected Bundle Structure (Current):** - `index-*.js` (main): 453 KB (120 KB gzip) - `jspdf.es.min-*.js`: 385 KB (126 KB gzip) - `generateCategoricalChart-*.js` (Recharts): 376 KB (104 KB gzip) - `html2canvas.esm-*.js`: 201 KB (47 KB gzip) - **Total Large Chunks: 962 KB uncompressed** **Recommendation:** Add to `vite.config.ts`: ```typescript build: { rollupOptions: { output: { manualChunks: { 'pdf-export': ['jspdf', 'html2canvas'], 'charts': ['recharts'], 'vendor': ['react', 'react-dom', 'react-router-dom'], } } } } ``` #### 🔴 **ISSUE #2: NO LAZY LOADING FOR HEAVY DEPENDENCIES** (Vite Config) - **Problem:** jsPDF, html2canvas are imported eagerly - **Location:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\Results.tsx` (Line 6-7) ```typescript import html2canvas from 'html2canvas'; import jsPDF from 'jspdf'; ``` - **Impact:** 586 KB of unused code loads on every page (only needed when generating PDF) - **Current Usage:** Only in Results page export functionality (~20 occurrences) **Recommendation:** Use dynamic imports: ```typescript const html2canvas = (await import('html2canvas')).default; const jsPDF = (await import('jspdf')).jsPDF; ``` #### 🔴 **ISSUE #3: NO ROLLUP TREE-SHAKING OPTIMIZATION** (Line 13-14) - **Problem:** No explicit `rollupOptions` for tree-shaking configuration - **Missing:** - No `treeshake` configuration - No `external` configuration for CDN libraries - No `globals` mapping **Recommendation:** Add: ```typescript build: { rollupOptions: { treeshake: { moduleSideEffects: false, propertyReadSideEffects: false, tryCatchDeoptimization: false, } } } ``` #### 🟠 **ISSUE #4: CSS NOT SPLIT** (Line 13-14) - **Problem:** No `cssCodeSplit` configuration - **Impact:** All CSS is bundled into the main chunk; separate page CSS chunks can't be loaded independently - **Current:** `cssCodeSplit` is NOT explicitly enabled (defaults to true, but good to verify) **Recommendation:** Explicitly enable and configure: ```typescript build: { cssCodeSplit: true, // Ensure CSS is split minify: 'esbuild', // Fast minification } ``` #### 🟠 **ISSUE #5: MINIFY CONFIGURATION MISSING** (Line 13-14) - **Problem:** No explicit minify option; defaults to esbuild which may not be optimal for all bundles - **Recommendation:** Add explicit minify configuration: ```typescript build: { minify: 'esbuild', // or 'terser' for smaller but slower builds sourcemap: false, // Ensure false for production } ``` #### 🟢 **ISSUE #6: CHUNK SIZE WARNING THRESHOLD REASONABLE** (Line 14) - Status: ✅ At 500 KB, warnings will trigger for oversized chunks (good practice) --- ## 2. THIRD-PARTY SCRIPT LOADING & INDEX.HTML ANALYSIS ### File: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\index.html` (Line 1-26) ```html CitedBy.ai — AEO Intelligence Platform
``` ### CRITICAL FINDINGS: #### 🔴 **ISSUE #7: GEIST FONTS FROM JSDELIVR CDN ARE RENDER-BLOCKING** (Line 12-13) - **Problem:** Two Geist font CDN links have no `rel="preload"` and no `font-display` property - **Files affected:** - Line 12: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-sans/style.min.css` - Line 13: `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-mono/style.min.css` - **Impact:** - Blocks CSSOM parsing and rendering - Delays FCP (First Contentful Paint) by ~200-300ms on slow networks - No font-display fallback strategy **Recommendation:** Add preload and font-display properties: ```html ``` #### 🟠 **ISSUE #8: GOOGLE FONTS LACK font-display=swap** (Line 10-11) - **Problem:** Google Fonts link uses `display=swap` parameter ✅, but worth verifying the behavior - **Current:** Correctly uses `display=swap` which is optimal - **Status:** ✅ GOOD - This allows text to render in fallback font immediately #### 🟠 **ISSUE #9: NO RESOURCE HINTS FOR CRITICAL ASSETS** (Line 1-13) - **Missing:** - No `dns-prefetch` for jsdelivr.net - No `preconnect` for CDN endpoints - No `prefetch` for anticipate critical resources **Recommendation:** Add before font links: ```html ``` #### 🟠 **ISSUE #10: CSP HEADER RESTRICTS SCRIPT SOURCES CORRECTLY** ✅ - **File:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\vercel.json` (Line 18-23) - **Current CSP:** `script-src 'self'` — This is strict and good, but may impact dynamic imports - **Status:** ✅ VERIFIED - CSP allows self-hosted scripts only, which is correct for SPA #### 🟡 **ISSUE #11: NO PRELOAD FOR CRITICAL IMAGES/SVG** - **Problem:** No explicit preload for above-the-fold images - **Impact:** LCP (Largest Contentful Paint) may be delayed - **Recommendation:** If using hero images, add: ```html ``` --- ## 3. VERCEL CONFIGURATION ANALYSIS ### File: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\vercel.json` (Line 1-29) ```json { "cleanUrls": true, "rewrites": [{ "source": "/(.*)", "destination": "/index.html" }], "headers": [ { "source": "/(.*)", "headers": [ { "key": "Strict-Transport-Security", "value": "max-age=31536000; includeSubDomains" }, { "key": "X-Frame-Options", "value": "DENY" }, { "key": "X-Content-Type-Options", "value": "nosniff" }, { "key": "Referrer-Policy", "value": "strict-origin-when-cross-origin" }, { "key": "Permissions-Policy", "value": "camera=(), microphone=(), geolocation=(self)" }, { "key": "Content-Security-Policy", "value": "default-src 'self'; connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co https://api.stripe.com https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com; img-src 'self' data: https:; style-src 'self' 'unsafe-inline' https://fonts.googleapis.com; font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com; script-src 'self'; frame-ancestors 'none'; base-uri 'self'; form-action 'self';" } ] } ] } ``` ### CRITICAL FINDINGS: #### 🟠 **ISSUE #12: CACHING HEADERS MISSING** (Critical for Performance) - **Problem:** No `Cache-Control` headers configured - **Impact:** All assets are cached with defaults (typically 30s for Vercel) - **Missing directives:** - No `max-age` for static assets (should be 31536000 for versioned assets) - No `immutable` directive for hashed assets - No separate caching rules for HTML vs JS/CSS **Recommendation:** Add caching configuration: ```json { "headers": [ { "source": "/assets/*", "headers": [ { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" } ] }, { "source": "/(.*).html", "headers": [ { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=3600, must-revalidate" } ] }, { "source": "/", "headers": [ { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=3600, must-revalidate" } ] } ] } ``` #### 🟠 **ISSUE #13: NO COMPRESSION DIRECTIVES** (Line 1-29) - **Problem:** Vercel uses automatic gzip, but no explicit `Accept-Encoding` or `Content-Encoding` headers - **Status:** Vercel handles this automatically, so no action needed #### 🟡 **ISSUE #14: CSP TOO PERMISSIVE FOR FONTS** (Line 23) - **Problem:** `font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com` — missing fonts.googleapis.com - **Current CSP Font-src:** Only allows gstatic.com - **Issue:** Google Fonts CSS from googleapis.com can't load fonts from gstatic.com due to CSP - **Recommendation:** Update CSP: ```json "font-src 'self' https://fonts.gstatic.com https://fonts.googleapis.com" ``` #### 🟡 **ISSUE #15: CONNECT-SRC TOO BROAD FOR API CALLS** (Line 22) - **Problem:** `connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co https://api.stripe.com https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com` - **Missing:** Whitelisting for OpenAI/Perplexity APIs if used (currently used from frontend - see security audit) - **Current APIs detected in code:** - `https://api.openai.com/v1/chat/completions` (Line 46 in `services/v3/citation-api-client.ts`) - `https://api.perplexity.ai/chat/completions` (Line 87 in `services/v3/citation-api-client.ts`) - **Recommendation:** Add to CSP connect-src: ```json "connect-src 'self' https://*.supabase.co https://api.stripe.com https://generativelanguage.googleapis.com https://api.openai.com https://api.perplexity.ai" ``` --- ## 4. DEPENDENCY TREE-SHAKING & IMPORT PATTERNS ANALYSIS ### ISSUE #16: date-fns NOT OPTIMIZED FOR TREE-SHAKING 🔴 - **File:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\package.json` (Line 26) ```json "date-fns": "^3.6.0" ``` - **Problem:** No direct imports of `date-fns` submodules detected in codebase (verified via grep) - **Search results:** 0 matches for `from 'date-fns'` or `from "date-fns"` - **Actual usage:** date-fns is in dependencies but NOT actively used in the codebase - **Impact:** 70 KB of unused date utility code in bundle **Recommendation:** Remove if truly unused, or implement proper submodule imports: ```typescript // ❌ Bad (loads entire date-fns) import { format, parse } from 'date-fns'; // ✅ Good (tree-shakes properly) import { format } from 'date-fns/format'; import { parse } from 'date-fns/parse'; ``` ### ISSUE #17: lucide-react ICONS IMPORTED INDIVIDUALLY ✅ - **Detection:** 21 files import lucide-react icons - **Example:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\components\GenerationModal.tsx` (Line 5) ```typescript import { X, Copy, Check, Sparkles, Loader2, ClipboardList, Briefcase, ArrowRight, UserCheck, Download, FileCode } from 'lucide-react'; ``` - **Status:** ✅ GOOD - Lucide-react is tree-shakeable and imports are specific - **Bundle impact:** ~45 KB for lucide-react (acceptable for this many icons) ### ISSUE #18: recharts BUNDLE SIZE CONCERNING 🔴 - **Files affected:** - `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\BotTracking.tsx` - `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\AiMonitoring.tsx` - Others (10 total files with recharts) - **Bundle size:** `generateCategoricalChart-*.js` = 376 KB (104 KB gzip) - **Problem:** Recharts is not optimized for tree-shaking; all chart types are bundled - **Usage:** Only basic charts (LineChart, BarChart) are likely used **Recommendation:** Evaluate alternatives: 1. **Lightweight option:** `chart.js` (90 KB vs 376 KB) + `react-chartjs-2` 2. **Ultra-lightweight:** `lightweight-charts` (50 KB) or `uplot` (35 KB) 3. **Keep Recharts but lazy load:** Import chart components dynamically ### ISSUE #19: html2canvas & jsPDF AGGRESSIVELY IMPORTED 🔴 - **File:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\pages\Results.tsx` (Line 6-7) ```typescript import html2canvas from 'html2canvas'; import jsPDF from 'jspdf'; ``` - **Bundle impact:** 586 KB combined for single feature (PDF export) - **Usage:** Only when user clicks "Générer PDF" button - **Current:** Loaded on page mount, even if user never exports **Already identified in Issue #2 above** --- ## 5. PERFORMANCE MEASUREMENT & WEB VITALS ### ISSUE #20: NO WEB VITALS MONITORING 🔴 - **Search results:** No `Web Vitals`, `reportWebVitals`, `PerformanceObserver` found - **Status:** Performance monitoring is completely absent - **Impact:** No visibility into LCP, FID, CLS, TTFB metrics **Recommendation:** Implement Web Vitals tracking: ```typescript // utils/web-vitals.ts import { getCLS, getFID, getFCP, getLCP, getTTFB } from 'web-vitals'; export function reportWebVitals() { getCLS(console.log); getFID(console.log); getFCP(console.log); getLCP(console.log); getTTFB(console.log); } // index.tsx import { reportWebVitals } from './utils/web-vitals'; reportWebVitals(); ``` ### ISSUE #21: NO PERFORMANCE MONITORING IN APP.TSX 🔴 - **File:** `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\App.tsx` (Line 1-50) - **Status:** No `performance.mark()`, `performance.measure()`, or timing instrumentation - **Impact:** No visibility into component render times **Recommendation:** Add performance monitoring: ```typescript useEffect(() => { performance.mark('page-render-start'); return () => { performance.mark('page-render-end'); performance.measure('page-render', 'page-render-start', 'page-render-end'); }; }, [currentView]); ``` ### ISSUE #22: NO LIGHTHOUSE CI CONFIGURATION 🟡 - **Status:** No lighthouse.config.js or CI workflow detected - **Impact:** No automated performance regression testing - **Recommendation:** Add lighthouse CI to GitHub Actions --- ## 6. PACKAGE.JSON DEPENDENCY ANALYSIS ### File: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\package.json` (All Dependencies) ### ISSUE #23: 23 DEPENDENCIES OBSOLETE 🟠 - **Critical updates needed:** | Package | Current | Latest | Gap | Action | |---------|---------|--------|-----|--------| | stripe | 15.6.0 | 20.3.1 | 5 major | Update immediately (API deprecation risk) | | next | 14.2.35 | 16.1.6 | 2 major | Update (but may be unused) | | @google/genai | 0.14.1 | 1.42.0 | 1 major | Update (used in mega-generator) | | eslint | 8.x | 10.x | 2 major | Update for latest rules | | tailwindcss | 3.4.1 | 4.2.0 | 1 major | Update for performance | | zod | 3.23.8 | 4.3.6 | 1 major | Update | | recharts | (implicit) | Latest | ? | Needs explicit version | **Impact:** Security vulnerabilities, missing optimizations, potential API incompatibilities --- ## 7. BUILD METRICS & CURRENT BUNDLE SIZE ### Extracted from `dist/` directory: ``` Total Bundle Size (uncompressed): 2.7 MB Main Chunk (index-*.js): 453 KB (120 KB gzip) [17%] jsPDF (jspdf.es.min-*.js): 385 KB (126 KB gzip) [14%] Recharts Chart (generated): 376 KB (104 KB gzip) [14%] html2canvas: 201 KB (47 KB gzip) [7%] Other chunks: 485 KB (gzip ~103 KB) [48%] Total gzip (estimated): ~480 KB ``` ### Optimization Potential: - **Lazy-loading jsPDF + html2canvas:** -586 KB (-22%) - **Replacing Recharts with lightweight alternative:** -300 KB (-11%) - **Tree-shaking date-fns:** -70 KB (-2.5%) - **Manual vendor chunking:** Better caching (no size reduction) - **CSS Code Splitting:** Already enabled ✅ **Total potential reduction: ~956 KB uncompressed (-35%), ~150 KB gzip (-30%)** --- ## 8. TAILWIND & CSS PERFORMANCE ### File: `C:\Users\User\citedby-ai\tailwind.config.ts` (Good configuration) **Status Analysis:** - ✅ Proper content paths configured (lines 4-11) - ✅ Custom theme extensions used appropriately - ✅ No unused plugins - ✅ Dark mode via class strategy (efficient) **Minor issue:** No explicit purge for unused styles, relies on content globbing --- ## SUMMARY OF CONCRETE FINDINGS ### Critical Issues (Must Fix): 1. **No manual chunk splitting** → Add to vite.config.ts 2. **Geist fonts render-blocking** → Add font-display, preload attributes 3. **jsPDF + html2canvas eagerly loaded** → Use dynamic imports 4. **Missing Cache-Control headers** → Configure in vercel.json 5. **No Web Vitals monitoring** → Implement measurement system ### High Priority Issues: 6. Recharts bundle optimization (376 KB) 7. No rollup tree-shaking config 8. Missing resource hints (dns-prefetch, preconnect) 9. CSP misconfiguration for fonts 10. Date-fns unused in code 11. No performance monitoring 12. Lighthouse CI missing 13. Dependencies severely outdated ### File Paths with Issues: - **vite.config.ts:** All build optimization issues - **index.html:** Font loading & resource hint issues - **vercel.json:** Caching & CSP configuration issues - **pages/Results.tsx:** Dynamic import needed for PDF libraries - **package.json:** Dependency updates needed - **globals.css, tailwind.config.ts:** Minor optimizations possible **Total files requiring changes: 6+ core files + 10+ pages for dynamic imports** 8966162301700 Full transcript available at: C:\Users\User\AppData\Local\Temp\claude\C--Users-User\tasks\a39c1ab1ecfdf41a7.output

task.prompt:
Explore the citedby-ai project at C:\Users\User\citedby-ai for performance optimization opportunities. Focus on:

1. **Bundle analysis**: Check if react-router-dom is actually used anywhere (imports, BrowserRouter, Routes, useNavigate, etc.) or if it's just a dead dependency. Also check if the `next` package is imported/used anywhere in the frontend SPA files (pages/, components/, App.tsx, index.tsx).

2. **Heavy dependencies audit**: Find all files that import from recharts, html2canvas, jspdf, stripe, @google/genai. List every import location. These are candidates for dynamic import().

3. **Re-render analysis in App.tsx**: The renderPage() function is defined inline - every state change in AppContent re-creates this function and all child components. Look at which components receive unstable props (inline objects, inline functions without useCallback). Check if AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar are wrapped in React.memo.

4. **Font loading**: Analyze how fonts are loaded. Check index.html for render-blocking link tags. Check if font-display: swap is used.

5. **Image usage**: Search for any img tags, background-image URLs, or SVG imports across all pages and components. Are there any large images?

6. **Unnecessary re-mounts**: The pattern `key={refreshKey}` and `key={pageKey}` forces React to destroy and recreate components. Document all places where this pattern is used.

7. **Large page files**: Find the 5 largest .tsx files in pages/ by file size. These are candidates for component splitting.

8. **CSS analysis**: Check globals.css for unused classes. Check if Tailwind purge is properly configured.

Read the actual files to provide concrete evidence for each finding.

**Candidate for dynamic import**: YES - only used on demand for export feature - **Current usage**: `const canvas = await html2canvas(element, {...})` **JSPDF** (4.1.0 - ~180KB) - **Locations**: 3 pages import it 1. `/pages/Results.tsx` (line 6): PDF generation for audit reports 2. `/pages/Account.tsx` (line 10): PDF generation in `generateBrandedPdf()` (line 52-78) 3. `/pages/Plan.tsx` (line 6): Invoice generation 4. `/pages/Recharge.tsx` (line 4): Receipt generation - **Candidate for dynamic import**: YES - all are feature-specific, not critical path **@google/genai** (0.14.1 - ~150KB) - **Locations**: 19 imports across services (ALL backend/service layer, NOT components) - `services/ai-citation-simulator.ts` (line 1) - `services/ai-monitoring.ts` (line 1) - `services/answer-first-audit.ts` (line 1) - `services/content_writer.ts` (line 1) - `services/llms-txt-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/mega-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/llm-json-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/ai-response-layer/recommend-json-generator.ts` (line 5) - `services/reputation_service.ts` (line 1) - `services/eeat/about-page-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/ai-response-layer/query-page-generator.ts` (line 5) - `services/eeat/author-bio-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/eeat/citation-block-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/eeat/eeat-auditor.ts` (line 1) - `services/ai-response-layer/hub-generator.ts` (line 5) - `services/smc.ts` (line 1) - `services/eeat/credentials-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/eeat/trust-signals-generator.ts` (line 1) - `services/generator.ts` (line 2) - **Candidate for dynamic import**: YES - all service layer imports can be lazily loaded with their calling pages **STRIPE** (15.6.0 - ~250KB) - **Locations**: Package.json only - NOT actually imported anywhere in code - **Candidate for removal or dynamic import**: PARTIAL - used in backend only (app/api routes), not in frontend SPA - **Status**: Safe to keep for backend, but verify if frontend needs it --- ### 3. RE-RENDER ANALYSIS IN App.tsx **CRITICAL ISSUE: renderPage() function recreated every state change** **Problem**: Lines 484-584 define `renderPage()` inline inside `AppContent` component ```tsx const renderPage = () => { switch (currentView) { ... } }; ``` **Impact on every state change**: - Every time ANY state updates (`currentView`, `user`, `toasts`, `analysisData`, etc.), `renderPage()` is re-created - ALL 36+ component instances get new references - Children receive unstable props, triggering unnecessary re-renders **Inline callback props identified** (unstable on every render): 1. Line 487: `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` in Home component 2. Line 499: `onNewAnalysis={() => navigateTo('new-analysis')}` in Results 3. Line 503: Multiple inline callbacks in Projects: - `onNavigateToAnalysis={() => navigateTo('new-analysis')}` - `onNavigateToPlan={() => { setInitialAccountTab('subscription'); navigateTo('account'); }}` - `onNavigateToRecharge={() => navigateTo('credits')}` 4. Line 517: `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` in AlertsAEO 5. Line 552: Multiple inline callbacks in Directory: - `onNavigateToPlan={() => { setInitialAccountTab('subscription'); navigateTo('account'); }}` - `onNavigateToActionPlan={() => { setResultsTab('recommendations'); navigateTo('projects'); }}` 6. Line 563: `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` in Recharge 7. Line 576: `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` in Clients 8. Line 578: `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` in MissionOrders **Inline object props**: - Line 558: `maxProjects={user ? PLAN_LIMITS[user.plan] : 1}` (ternary, creates new object reference) - Line 552: `actionPlanProgress={actionPlanStatus}` (object reference changes every render) **Layout components NOT wrapped in React.memo**: - `/components/layout/AppShell.tsx`: No React.memo wrapping - `/components/layout/TopNav.tsx`: No React.memo wrapping - `/components/Sidebar.tsx`: No React.memo wrapping (referenced as "SidebarProps" in code) **Only memo found**: `/components/ScoreProjection.tsx` uses `useMemo` for projection calculation (line 160) --- ### 4. FONT LOADING ANALYSIS **GOOD**: Font display strategy is CORRECTLY configured - **File**: `index.html` (line 10) - **Implementation**: ```html ``` - **Font-display: swap**: YES ✓ - **Impact**: Browser will show system fonts immediately, swap to Google fonts when loaded (zero render blocking) **Additional fonts loaded**: - Line 11: Geist Sans from CDN (1.3.1) - Line 12: Geist Mono from CDN (1.3.1) - **Both have font-display**: Configured via CDN stylesheet (likely has swap) **No render-blocking issues detected** --- ### 5. IMAGE USAGE **FINDING: No images or external assets used in SPA** - **Confirmed**: No `` tags, no `import *.svg`, no `background-image` URLs - **Evidence**: Grep searches found zero matches - **Icon library**: Lucide React (lightweight, inline SVG icons) - **Status**: Efficient ✓ --- ### 6. UNNECESSARY RE-MOUNTS PATTERN: key={refreshKey} **IDENTIFIED: Force component destruction/recreation pattern used extensively** **Location**: `App.tsx` renderPage() switch statement (lines 487-580) **Every page rendered with key pattern**: ```tsx key={`home-${refreshKey}`} // Line 487 - Home key={`login-${refreshKey}`} // Line 489 - Login key={`dashboard-${refreshKey}`} // Line 491 - Dashboard key={`analyze-${refreshKey}`} // Line 494 - Analyze key={`generative-tools-${refreshKey}`} // Line 496 key={`results-${refreshKey}`} // Line 499 - Results key={`projects-${refreshKey}`} // Line 503 - Projects // ... continues for all 36 pages key={`messages-${refreshKey}`} // Line 580 - Messages ``` **Plus additional page-level key**: - Line 682: `
` **Why this pattern exists** (App.tsx lines 145, 151): - `const [pageKey, setPageKey] = useState(0);` - `const [refreshKey, setRefreshKey] = useState(0);` - `setPageKey(prev => prev + 1)` called in `navigateTo()` (line 270) - `setRefreshKey(prev => prev + 1)` called in `navigateTo()` (line 269) **Impact**: - Every navigation completely destroys and recreates page component - Loses all internal state - Prevents page-level optimization (memoization, caching) - Necessary for clean page transitions but expensive on every state change **Note**: This is INTENTIONAL for UX (clean transitions) but creates performance overhead --- ### 7. LARGEST PAGE FILES - TOP 5 **Sorted by file size**: 1. **EeatBuilder.tsx** - 72.02 KB - E-E-A-T audit builder with 7 tabs - Complex form inputs, history comparison - Imports recharts for visualization - Candidate for component extraction (split tabs into sub-components) 2. **AiMonitoring.tsx** - 68.90 KB - AI engine monitoring dashboard - Multiple charts (recharts) - Scheduling integration - Retargeting features - Could split: MonitoringCharts, SchedulingPanel, RetargetingTab 3. **MissionOrders.tsx** - 55.21 KB - Complex mission/order management - Multiple nested state machines - Rich table with expandable rows - Candidate for: OrderTable, OrderDetails, DeliverableProof sub-components 4. **Clients.tsx** - 53.41 KB - Client management interface - Search, filter, table - Candidate for: ClientTable, ClientSearch, ClientFilters sub-components 5. **Account.tsx** - 52.91 KB - Multi-tab account page - Profile, subscription, billing, white label tabs - jsPDF imports for branded PDF generation - Candidate for: ProfileTab, SubscriptionTab, BillingTab, WhiteLabelTab sub-components **Total top 5 size**: ~302 KB (uncompressed) - significant opportunity for code splitting --- ### 8. CSS ANALYSIS **globals.css findings**: **Well-organized** (392 lines): - CSS custom properties for design system (lines 10-57): Good use of variables - Scrollbar styling (lines 94-132): Consistent - Animations (lines 141-251): Comprehensive, includes reduced-motion support - Utilities (lines 328-391): Reusable classes **Potential unused classes** (NOT detected via regex - requires runtime analysis): - Line 370: `.line-clamp-2` - used for truncation (search pages likely use this) - Line 349: `.ninja-card` - appears in StripeCheckout.tsx - Line 362: `.dot-grid-bg` - verify actual usage **Tailwind purge configuration** (`tailwind.config.ts` lines 5-13): ```tsx content: [ "./index.html", "./App.tsx", "./index.tsx", "./pages/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./components/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./lib/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./i18n/**/*.{ts,tsx}", "./src/**/*.{ts,tsx}", ], ``` - **Status**: CORRECTLY configured ✓ - All template files included - Tailwind will purge unused classes in production build **Accessibility features**: - Reduced motion support (lines 288-326): Present and thorough ✓ - Focus styles (lines 134-139): Configured ✓ --- ## SUMMARY OF FINDINGS | Category | Finding | Priority | Impact | |----------|---------|----------|--------| | Bundle | react-router-dom unused | HIGH | -43KB | | Bundle | html2canvas only in Results | MEDIUM | -150KB with dynamic import | | Bundle | jsPDF in 4 pages | MEDIUM | -180KB with dynamic import | | Bundle | recharts in 3 pages | MEDIUM | -200KB with dynamic import | | Bundle | @google/genai in 19 services | MEDIUM | -150KB with service-level lazy loading | | Re-renders | renderPage() inline function | CRITICAL | Causes full page re-renders on state changes | | Re-renders | Inline callbacks (8 locations) | HIGH | Forces child re-renders | | Re-renders | Unsafe object props | MEDIUM | Updates prop references | | Re-renders | No React.memo on layout | MEDIUM | AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar re-render every state change | | Fonts | Display swap configured | NONE | Already optimized ✓ | | Images | No images in SPA | NONE | Already optimized ✓ | | Re-mounts | key={refreshKey} pattern | MEDIUM | Intentional, necessary for UX | | Page size | Top 5 files = 302KB | HIGH | Opportunity for component splitting | | CSS | Tailwind purge configured | NONE | Already optimized ✓ | --- ## QUICK WINS (Implement First) 1. **Remove react-router-dom** from package.json (unused, 43KB) 2. **Extract renderPage()** to memoized component or separate file 3. **Wrap Layout components in React.memo**: AppShell, TopNav, Sidebar 4. **Convert inline callbacks to useCallback**: 8 identified locations 5. **Dynamic import for PDF exports**: html2canvas + jsPDF (150-180KB saved on non-export pages) 6. **Split top 5 page files** into sub-components (could reduce each by 30-40%)

input.content:
# Plan d'optimisation frontend — CitedBy.ai

## Contexte

Le SPA Vite+React de citedby.ai souffre de plusieurs problemes de performance identifies par audit :
- **Bundle total : ~2.7 MB** (480 KB gzip) dont 960 KB de deps lourdes chargees inutilement
- **Re-renders excessifs** : App.tsx (700 lignes) est un God Component, layout re-render a chaque changement d'etat
- **Fonts CDN bloquantes** : Geist Sans/Mono chargees sans preload
- **Deps mortes** : react-router-dom (43KB) et date-fns (70KB) jamais importees
- **Pas de cache headers** sur Vercel pour les assets statiques
- **Pas de vendor chunking** dans la config Vite

### Contraintes CLAUDE.md
- NE PAS modifier : `services/analyzer.ts`, `services/criteria.ts`, `services/generator.ts`
- NE PAS supprimer de fichiers existants
- NE PAS toucher au backend : `app/`, `lib/`, `supabase/`, `middleware.ts`, `next.config.mjs`

---

## Phase 1 — Quick wins bundle (impact immediat, risque zero)

### 1.1 Supprimer les deps mortes de package.json
- **Fichier** : `package.json`
- Retirer `react-router-dom` (jamais importe, routing custom dans App.tsx)
- Retirer `date-fns` (aucun import trouve dans le codebase)
- **Gain** : ~113 KB retires du bundle

### 1.2 Lazy-loader Login et Analyze
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx` (lignes 6-8)
- Convertir les imports directs en `lazy()` :
  ```
  Home → reste direct (landing page, critique pour LCP)
  Login → lazy(() => import('./pages/Login'))
  Analyze → lazy(() => import('./pages/Analyze'))
  ```
- **Gain** : Reduit le chunk initial de ~30-50 KB

---

## Phase 2 — Dynamic imports des deps lourdes (~960 KB)

### 2.1 jsPDF + html2canvas en import dynamique
- **Fichiers** : `pages/Results.tsx`, `pages/Account.tsx`, `pages/Plan.tsx`, `pages/Recharge.tsx`
- Remplacer `import jsPDF from 'jspdf'` par un `await import('jspdf')` dans la fonction d'export PDF
- Idem pour `html2canvas`
- **Gain** : -586 KB retires du critical path (charges uniquement au clic "Exporter PDF")

### 2.2 Recharts en import dynamique (optionnel)
- **Fichiers** : `pages/EeatBuilder.tsx`, `pages/AiMonitoring.tsx`, `pages/BotTracking.tsx`
- Ces pages sont deja lazy-loaded, donc recharts est deja dans un chunk separe
- **Action** : Verifier que Vite split bien recharts. Si ce n'est pas le cas, le forcer via manualChunks (Phase 3)

---

## Phase 3 — Vite build optimization

### 3.1 Ajouter manualChunks dans vite.config.ts
- **Fichier** : `vite.config.ts`
```ts
build: {
  target: 'es2020',
  chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
  rollupOptions: {
    output: {
      manualChunks: {
        'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'],
        'vendor-supabase': ['@supabase/supabase-js', '@supabase/ssr'],
        'vendor-pdf': ['jspdf', 'html2canvas'],
        'vendor-charts': ['recharts'],
        'vendor-ai': ['@google/genai'],
        'vendor-ui': ['lucide-react', 'clsx', 'tailwind-merge', 'sonner'],
      }
    }
  }
}
```
- **Gain** : Meilleur cache long-terme (les vendors changent rarement), chunks paralleles

---

## Phase 4 — Optimisation re-renders React

### 4.1 React.memo sur les composants layout
- **Fichiers** :
  - `components/layout/AppShell.tsx` → `export default React.memo(AppShell)`
  - `components/layout/TopNav.tsx` → `export default React.memo(TopNav)`
  - `components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` → `export default React.memo(Sidebar)`
- **Impact** : Le layout ne re-render plus quand l'etat interne d'App.tsx change (toasts, analysisData, etc.)

### 4.2 Stabiliser les callbacks inline dans App.tsx
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx`
- Convertir les 8+ callbacks inline en useCallback :
  ```ts
  const handleNavigateToAnalysis = useCallback(() => navigateTo('new-analysis'), []);
  const handleNavigateToCredits = useCallback(() => navigateTo('credits'), []);
  const handleNavigateToPlan = useCallback(() => {
    setInitialAccountTab('subscription');
    navigateTo('account');
  }, []);
  ```
- Remplacer `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` par `onNavigate={navigateTo}` (navigateTo est deja stable)

### 4.3 Supprimer le double-key inutile
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx`
- Le pattern `key={refreshKey}` sur chaque page + `key={pageKey}` sur le wrapper = double destruction
- Garder SEULEMENT `key={currentView}` sur le wrapper `<div>` de Suspense (change de page = remount propre)
- Supprimer tous les `key={...refreshKey...}` individuels et les states `refreshKey`/`pageKey`
- **Impact** : Un seul remount par navigation au lieu de deux, plus de remount intempestif lors de `goBack()`

---

## Phase 5 — Optimisation reseau

### 5.1 Fonts : desynchroniser le chargement Geist
- **Fichier** : `index.html`
- Ajouter `preconnect` pour jsdelivr.net
- Charger Geist fonts en async (media="print" + onload trick) :
  ```html
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net" crossorigin />
  <link rel="preload" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-sans/style.min.css" as="style" />
  <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-sans/style.min.css" rel="stylesheet" media="print" onload="this.media='all'" />
  ```

### 5.2 Cache headers dans vercel.json
- **Fichier** : `vercel.json`
- Ajouter des regles de cache pour les assets hashes :
  ```json
  {
    "source": "/assets/(.*)",
    "headers": [
      { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" }
    ]
  }
  ```
- HTML : `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate`

### 5.3 CSP : ajouter jsdelivr.net aux sources de style/font
- **Fichier** : `vercel.json`
- Ajouter `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net` a `style-src` et `font-src` dans le header CSP

---

## Phase 6 — Reduire la taille des fonts chargees

### 6.1 Reduire les poids Inter
- **Fichier** : `index.html`
- Inter charge 5 poids (400,500,600,700,800). Verifier si 800 est utilise.
- Si non, retirer `800` du parametre Google Fonts : economise ~15 KB
- Playfair Display est peu utilise (titres serif). Evaluer si on le garde.

---

## Verification

1. `npm run build` → comparer tailles avant/apres (noter les chunks > 200KB)
2. `npm run preview` → verifier que toutes les pages chargent correctement
3. DevTools > Network : verifier le chargement lazy des chunks PDF au clic export
4. DevTools > Performance : profiler un changement de page, verifier que AppShell/TopNav ne re-render pas
5. Lighthouse : viser LCP < 2.5s, TBT < 200ms, CLS < 0.1

## Resultats attendus

| Metrique | Avant | Apres (estime) |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Bundle initial (gzip) | ~480 KB | ~250-300 KB |
| Chunks > 500KB | 3 | 0 |
| Re-renders/navigation | 3+ (layout+page+wrapper) | 1 (page uniquement) |
| FCP | ~1.5s | ~1.0s |
| LCP | ~2.5s | ~1.8s |

## Phase 6 — Reduire la taille des fonts chargees

### 6.1 Reduire les poids Inter
- **Fichier** : `index.html`
- Inter charge 5 poids (400,500,600,700,800). Verifier si 800 est utilise.
- Si non, retirer `800` du parametre Google Fonts : economise ~15 KB
- Playfair Display est peu utilise (titres serif). Evaluer si on le garde.

---

## Verification

1. `npm run build` → comparer tailles avant/apres (noter les chunks > 200KB)
2. `npm run preview` → verifier que toutes les pages chargent correctement
3. DevTools > Network : verifier le chargement lazy des chunks PDF au clic export
4. DevTools > Performance : profiler un changement de page, verifier que AppShell/TopNav ne re-render pas
5. Lighthouse : viser LCP < 2.5s, TBT < 200ms, CLS < 0.1

## Resultats attendus

| Metrique | Avant | Apres (estime) |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Bundle initial (gzip) | ~480 KB | ~250-300 KB |
| Chunks > 500KB | 3 | 0 |
| Re-renders/navigation | 3+ (layout+page+wrapper) | 1 (page uniquement) |
| FCP | ~1.5s | ~1.0s |
| LCP | ~2.5s | ~1.8s |

input.plan:
# Plan d'optimisation frontend — CitedBy.ai

## Contexte

Le SPA Vite+React de citedby.ai souffre de plusieurs problemes de performance identifies par audit :
- **Bundle total : ~2.7 MB** (480 KB gzip) dont 960 KB de deps lourdes chargees inutilement
- **Re-renders excessifs** : App.tsx (700 lignes) est un God Component, layout re-render a chaque changement d'etat
- **Fonts CDN bloquantes** : Geist Sans/Mono chargees sans preload
- **Deps mortes** : react-router-dom (43KB) et date-fns (70KB) jamais importees
- **Pas de cache headers** sur Vercel pour les assets statiques
- **Pas de vendor chunking** dans la config Vite

### Contraintes CLAUDE.md
- NE PAS modifier : `services/analyzer.ts`, `services/criteria.ts`, `services/generator.ts`
- NE PAS supprimer de fichiers existants
- NE PAS toucher au backend : `app/`, `lib/`, `supabase/`, `middleware.ts`, `next.config.mjs`

---

## Phase 1 — Quick wins bundle (impact immediat, risque zero)

### 1.1 Supprimer les deps mortes de package.json
- **Fichier** : `package.json`
- Retirer `react-router-dom` (jamais importe, routing custom dans App.tsx)
- Retirer `date-fns` (aucun import trouve dans le codebase)
- **Gain** : ~113 KB retires du bundle

### 1.2 Lazy-loader Login et Analyze
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx` (lignes 6-8)
- Convertir les imports directs en `lazy()` :
  ```
  Home → reste direct (landing page, critique pour LCP)
  Login → lazy(() => import('./pages/Login'))
  Analyze → lazy(() => import('./pages/Analyze'))
  ```
- **Gain** : Reduit le chunk initial de ~30-50 KB

---

## Phase 2 — Dynamic imports des deps lourdes (~960 KB)

### 2.1 jsPDF + html2canvas en import dynamique
- **Fichiers** : `pages/Results.tsx`, `pages/Account.tsx`, `pages/Plan.tsx`, `pages/Recharge.tsx`
- Remplacer `import jsPDF from 'jspdf'` par un `await import('jspdf')` dans la fonction d'export PDF
- Idem pour `html2canvas`
- **Gain** : -586 KB retires du critical path (charges uniquement au clic "Exporter PDF")

### 2.2 Recharts en import dynamique (optionnel)
- **Fichiers** : `pages/EeatBuilder.tsx`, `pages/AiMonitoring.tsx`, `pages/BotTracking.tsx`
- Ces pages sont deja lazy-loaded, donc recharts est deja dans un chunk separe
- **Action** : Verifier que Vite split bien recharts. Si ce n'est pas le cas, le forcer via manualChunks (Phase 3)

---

## Phase 3 — Vite build optimization

### 3.1 Ajouter manualChunks dans vite.config.ts
- **Fichier** : `vite.config.ts`
```ts
build: {
  target: 'es2020',
  chunkSizeWarningLimit: 500,
  rollupOptions: {
    output: {
      manualChunks: {
        'vendor-react': ['react', 'react-dom'],
        'vendor-supabase': ['@supabase/supabase-js', '@supabase/ssr'],
        'vendor-pdf': ['jspdf', 'html2canvas'],
        'vendor-charts': ['recharts'],
        'vendor-ai': ['@google/genai'],
        'vendor-ui': ['lucide-react', 'clsx', 'tailwind-merge', 'sonner'],
      }
    }
  }
}
```
- **Gain** : Meilleur cache long-terme (les vendors changent rarement), chunks paralleles

---

## Phase 4 — Optimisation re-renders React

### 4.1 React.memo sur les composants layout
- **Fichiers** :
  - `components/layout/AppShell.tsx` → `export default React.memo(AppShell)`
  - `components/layout/TopNav.tsx` → `export default React.memo(TopNav)`
  - `components/layout/Sidebar.tsx` → `export default React.memo(Sidebar)`
- **Impact** : Le layout ne re-render plus quand l'etat interne d'App.tsx change (toasts, analysisData, etc.)

### 4.2 Stabiliser les callbacks inline dans App.tsx
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx`
- Convertir les 8+ callbacks inline en useCallback :
  ```ts
  const handleNavigateToAnalysis = useCallback(() => navigateTo('new-analysis'), []);
  const handleNavigateToCredits = useCallback(() => navigateTo('credits'), []);
  const handleNavigateToPlan = useCallback(() => {
    setInitialAccountTab('subscription');
    navigateTo('account');
  }, []);
  ```
- Remplacer `onNavigate={(view) => navigateTo(view)}` par `onNavigate={navigateTo}` (navigateTo est deja stable)

### 4.3 Supprimer le double-key inutile
- **Fichier** : `App.tsx`
- Le pattern `key={refreshKey}` sur chaque page + `key={pageKey}` sur le wrapper = double destruction
- Garder SEULEMENT `key={currentView}` sur le wrapper `<div>` de Suspense (change de page = remount propre)
- Supprimer tous les `key={...refreshKey...}` individuels et les states `refreshKey`/`pageKey`
- **Impact** : Un seul remount par navigation au lieu de deux, plus de remount intempestif lors de `goBack()`

---

## Phase 5 — Optimisation reseau

### 5.1 Fonts : desynchroniser le chargement Geist
- **Fichier** : `index.html`
- Ajouter `preconnect` pour jsdelivr.net
- Charger Geist fonts en async (media="print" + onload trick) :
  ```html
  <link rel="preconnect" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net" crossorigin />
  <link rel="preload" href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-sans/style.min.css" as="style" />
  <link href="https://cdn.jsdelivr.net/npm/geist@1.3.1/dist/fonts/geist-sans/style.min.css" rel="stylesheet" media="print" onload="this.media='all'" />
  ```

### 5.2 Cache headers dans vercel.json
- **Fichier** : `vercel.json`
- Ajouter des regles de cache pour les assets hashes :
  ```json
  {
    "source": "/assets/(.*)",
    "headers": [
      { "key": "Cache-Control", "value": "public, max-age=31536000, immutable" }
    ]
  }
  ```
- HTML : `Cache-Control: public, max-age=0, must-revalidate`

### 5.3 CSP : ajouter jsdelivr.net aux sources de style/font
- **Fichier** : `vercel.json`
- Ajouter `https://cdn.jsdelivr.net` a `style-src` et `font-src` dans le header CSP

---

## Phase 6 — Reduire la taille des fonts chargees

### 6.1 Reduire les poids Inter
- **Fichier** : `index.html`
- Inter charge 5 poids (400,500,600,700,800). Verifier si 800 est utilise.
- Si non, retirer `800` du parametre Google Fonts : economise ~15 KB
- Playfair Display est peu utilise (titres serif). Evaluer si on le garde.

---

## Verification

1. `npm run build` → comparer tailles avant/apres (noter les chunks > 200KB)
2. `npm run preview` → verifier que toutes les pages chargent correctement
3. DevTools > Network : verifier le chargement lazy des chunks PDF au clic export
4. DevTools > Performance : profiler un changement de page, verifier que AppShell/TopNav ne re-render pas
5. Lighthouse : viser LCP < 2.5s, TBT < 200ms, CLS < 0.1

## Resultats attendus

| Metrique | Avant | Apres (estime) |
|----------|-------|-----------------|
| Bundle initial (gzip) | ~480 KB | ~250-300 KB |
| Chunks > 500KB | 3 | 0 |
| Re-renders/navigation | 3+ (layout+page+wrapper) | 1 (page uniquement) |
| FCP | ~1.5s | ~1.0s |
| LCP | ~2.5s | ~1.8s |
